title: Binance Faces EU Service Limits Next Week as MiCA Rules Take Effect
summary: Binance will limit EU onboarding and services from July 1 under MiCA rules after failing to gain authorization from a member state, while withdrawals remain available for users.
body:
Binance, the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange, will begin restricting services for European Union users starting July 1. This move comes as the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulations take full effect, requiring exchanges to gain authorization from an EU member state. Binance has not secured such authorization, leading to these restrictions.

Under the new rules, Binance will limit onboarding for new users in the EU and restrict certain services. However, existing users will still be able to withdraw their funds. The exchange has not provided a timeline for when it might regain full compliance with MiCA, leaving many users in limbo.

This development is significant for EU-based crypto users, as Binance has been a popular platform for trading and managing digital assets. The restrictions highlight the growing regulatory scrutiny facing crypto exchanges and the importance of compliance with regional laws. Users may need to seek alternative platforms that are fully compliant with MiCA.

For those affected, the immediate next step is to review their Binance accounts and consider transferring funds to other platforms if needed. It's also advisable to stay updated on any announcements from Binance regarding potential compliance solutions or changes in service availability. Watch for further developments as the crypto industry adapts to MiCA's stringent requirements.
twitterThread: Binance is restricting EU services starting July 1 due to MiCA compliance issues. Existing users can still withdraw funds, but new onboarding is limited.
The EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulations require exchanges to gain authorization from an EU member state. Binance hasn't secured this, leading to service cuts.

If you're an EU Binance user, review your account and consider transferring funds. Stay updated on Binance's compliance efforts. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-25-binance-to-restrict-eu-services-starting-july-1-under-mica-rules
